description: The number and apparent-gender make-up of the participants in a pose, and their roles. Adult media conventionally encodes multi-person composition with letter codes (M = apparent male, F = apparent female), e.g. MMF or FFM; that convention is reproduced here for interoperability with existing adult-industry and booru-style tagging. Codes describe apparent on-camera presentation only and imply nothing about identity. This axis is orthogonal to pose structure: the same structural position can occur across several compositions. Adults only.

dimensions.description: Number of participants in the depiction.

dimensions.scale_citation: Adult-industry / booru letter-code convention (M = apparent male, F = apparent female); describes apparent presentation only.
dimensions.description: Apparent-gender make-up of the participants, in the conventional letter-code form.

values.definition: One apparent-male and one apparent-female participant.
Four or more, mixed or unspecified make-up.

dimensions.scale_citation: Count of partners in the receiving role in the depicted act.
dimensions.description: How many participants are in the receiving role.

dimensions.scale_citation: Count of partners in the penetrating role in the depicted act.
dimensions.description: How many participants are in the penetrating role.

aggregation_notes.note: Composition codes describe apparent on-camera presentation, not participant identity or orientation.

references: Danbooru tag group: sex_acts — participant-composition letter codes (MMF/FFM/…) and solo/duo/group tags.
e621 tagging system — solo / duo / group participant tags.
